property_specification: In Loiter Mode the Copter maintains its altitude.
Once the copter has landed, the motors will eventually be disarmed.
If the altitude exceeds the maximum or minimum limit, then a fence breach will eventually be declared in all modes.
The vehicle must always maintain a distance greater than or equal to FENCE_MARGIN from the fence boundary to prevent a breach.
FENCE_RADIUS must be larger than FENCE_MARGIN.
If either the GPS arming check or the EKF failsafe is disabled, the fence must be disabled.
If the fence is enabled, arming the vehicle eventually requires GPS lock to be acquired first.
If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and you lose GPS lock while flying, then the Fence will eventually be disabled.
If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and the vehicle is in an autonomous mode, the vehicle will eventually switch to LAND mode.
Assuming that the FENCE_ACTION is not set to RTL, if the vehicle breaches an exclusion fence in Loiter mode for Copter and Object Avoidance is setup, then eventually the vehicle will stop.
If the vehicle reaches either the polygon fence or cylindrical fence, then the failsafe behavior (stop or RTL) will eventually trigger
If FENCE_TYPE is 2, inclusion zones overlap, a boundary is crossed, and FENCE_OPTIONS bit 1 is not set, then a fence breach will eventually occur.
Whenever the copter is in Loiter, PosHold, or AltHold modes and altitude reaches FENCE_ALT_MAX, it does not increase altitude further.
uppaal_formula: A[] ((mode == Loiter || mode == PosHold || mode == AltHold) && altitude >= FENCE_ALT_MAX) imply (!increasing_altitude)

property_specification: Whenever the system is in Loiter mode and exceeds the boundary, a breach is eventually declared.
If the system is in a mode other than the Loiter mode and either the altitude is reached or the boundary is reached, then it will eventually execute FENCE_ACTION.
Whenever Object Avoidance is enabled and RTL is active, the system eventually avoids fences.
If the vehicle's distance exceeds 100 meters, it will eventually switch to LAND/HOLD mode.
If FENCE_ACTION is 6 or 7, FENCE_RET_ALT is zero, and altitude limits are non-zero, altitude equals midway between limits.
If FENCE_ACTION is set to a guided return mode (6 or 7), then the altitude at which it returns is determined by FENCE_RET_ALT.
Mode changes cannot be executed after the fence breach unless the FENCE_OPTIONS bit 0 is set to “0”.
Whenever the PWM value is greater than 1800, all configured fences are enabled.
If the system is on the ground, disarmed, and the Minimum Altitude fence is enabled, then a pre-arm failure will eventually occur.
In Loiter mode, Copter always maintains an altitude less than or equal to FENCE_ALT_MAX.
If the system is inside the fence, the breach_status field is 0.
The system can eventually perform a failsafe action.
If the copter is in ALTHOLD or LOITER mode and objects are detected in any quadrant, it will eventually move away from the threat or stop.
If PRX1_TYPE is set to 4, the proximity sensor is enabled.
The system can possibly be in the Loiter state.
Whenever an obstacle approaches and the sensor detects it, the vehicle will eventually back away.
The vehicle always maintains a distance of at least OA_MARGIN_MAX (2m) from obstacles.
Whenever the vehicle's altitude above home is below OA_DB_ALT_MIN within a 3-meter radius around home, obstacles are rejected.
Surface Tracking is possible when the system is in Loiter, PosHold, or AltHold modes.
If the system is using GCS provided terrain data, the TERRAIN_ENABLE parameter is set to 1.
Whenever the system is in AUTO mode, the mission will eventually be executed
The parameter EK3_RNG_USE_HGT must always be -1.
If terrain data is unavailable during RTL with TERRAIN_FOLLOW enabled, altitude interpretation defaults to home.
If during RTL, WPNAV_RFND_USE is 1, RTL_ALT is within the rangefinder's operating range, and the rangefinder is healthy, then the rangefinder is used instead of the terrain database.
If the vehicle is executing a mission requiring terrain data, unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, and is flying, it will eventually switch to RTL mode.
If the vehicle is executing a mission requiring terrain data, unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, and is landed, it will eventually disarm.
Whenever the vehicle climbs out of the rangefinder’s range, the pilot’s stick returns to directly controlling the vehicle’s target climb rate.
Whenever inverted flight is enabled (RCx_OPTION switch 43 activated), the vehicle eventually stabilizes with a roll of 180 degrees.
Eventually, in extreme cases, EKF failsafes might occur.
If INS_HNTCH_OPTS does not has bit 1 set, then the average frequency of all motors will set the center frequency.
If the system is in ESC Telemetry Mode, then each motor will eventually have a notch assigned at its RPM.
If the system is in Throttle Mode, then each motor will eventually have a notch assigned at its throttle input.
If any RPM source (Sensor, ESC telem, FFT) fails, the system will eventually use the minimum frequency (FFT_MINHZ, INS_HNTCH_FREQ, or INS_HNTC2_FREQ) for the notch center frequency.

If the autopilot is capable of running FFT (CapableFFT), and FFT_ENABLE is enabled, and RC Auxiliary function 162 is used (RC_Aux_162_Used) during sustained hover (Hovering), then the parameters (INS_HNTCH_FREQ, INS_HNTCH_ENABLE, INS_HNTCH_MODE) will eventually be automatically set (ParametersSet).
If the harmonic notch is enabled (enabled == 1), then either INS_HNTCH_ENABLE or INS_HNTC2_ENABLE must be set to 1.
Setting INS_HNTCH_MODE or INS_HNTC2_MODE to 2 or 5 leads to using an RPM sensor to set the harmonic notch frequency.
The notch's center frequency is always greater than or equal to INS_HNTC2_FREQ when set below hover frequency.
If INS_HNTCH_REF or INS_HNTC2_REF is 1, then scaling of the harmonic notch will be disabled.
Setting INS_HNTCH_MODE to 3 or INS_HNTC2_MODE to 3 leads to ESC telemetry being selected
If INS_HNTCH_MODE or INS_HNTC2_MODE is equal to 4, then the system uses the FFT detected frequency for controlling the harmonic notch frequency.
If FFT_ENABLE is 0, then the FFT engine is disabled.
It is always possible to switch back to Stabilize/Alt-Hold.
Setting GPS1_TYPE to 0 leads to the GPS being disabled.
Whenever GPS1_TYPE is 0, the GPS is disabled
When RC6_OPTION = 90 and the switch is in the high position (Source3), the EKF will eventually lose its position estimate and trigger an EKF failsafe.
If ARMING_CHECK is set to -9, the GPS arming check is disabled.
Setting SERIAL1_BAUD to 921 leads to SERIAL1's baud rate eventually becoming 921600
If SERIAL1_PROTOCOL is set to 13, the system can read the IndoorLoiter2 protocol.
Whenever the system is in GUIDED or AUTO mode and GUID_OPTIONS bit 7 is 1 or AUTO_OPTIONS bit 7 is 1 and WVANE_ENABLE is non-zero, active weathervaning is active.
uppaal_formula: (mode == GUIDED || mode == AUTO) && (GUID_OPTIONS bit 7 == 1 || AUTO_OPTIONS bit 7 == 1) && WVANE_ENABLE != 0 --> weathervaning_active

property_specification: If pilot yaw control is enabled and pilot input ceases, the vehicle will eventually yaw back to the Weathervaning Direction.
Whenever the ground speed is below WVANE_SPD_MAX, weathervaning is permitted
Whenever GPS lock is shown, position has eventually become valid.
When the system is in the Disarmed state, GPS lock is acquired.
Eventually, the system is ready to fly when armed and a GPS lock is acquired.
When the system is in the Safety state, a pre-arm error condition is generated.
When the system is in the Safety state, servo outputs have their PWM disabled.
Whenever the safety switch is pressed, the motors and servos are eventually able to move once the vehicle is armed.
The safety button is only active when disarmed, which leads to the system entering the safety on state when disarmed.
Whenever the external safety switch is activated, the system eventually enters a safe state.
It is possible to arm and operate in ACRO or STABILIZE modes.
Operation without an RC system is possible.
There exists a path where a Copter or Rover mode operates without GPS and Compass.
If throttle is held down and rudder is held right for 5 seconds, then the copter becomes armed.
If the system fails to Arm with throttle down and to the right and the motors are not spinning, then the Pre-Arm Safety Check has failed.
If an output's function is set to RCPassThru, then it corresponds to an RC input.
The vehicle can potentially reach a state of inverted flight.
The tail servo's PWM value must never exceed SERVO7_MAX to prevent binding. The servo's PWM value must never be less than SERVO1_MIN to avoid physical limits.
The vehicle can be armed only when in Stabilize mode.
Whenever the vehicle is armed and not flying, the motors spin at MOT_SPIN_ARM speed.
MOT_SPIN_MIN must always be at least 0.03 higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM
Once the vehicle enters a failsafe mode (Radio/Battery/GCS/Terrain Data Loss) involving a mode change, it remains in that mode until the pilot directly changes the mode.
If the radio failsafe is enabled and set up correctly, then whenever any condition occurs for more than RC_FS_TIMEOUT seconds, the failsafe will eventually trigger.
After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to do nothing.
After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to land immediately.
After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to execute RTL.
Always, if the copter is disarmed, then failsafe does not occur.
Whenever the copter is armed and in Stabilize or Acro mode and throttle input is at minimum, it eventually disarms.
When the copter enters RTL mode due to RC falisafe, it remains in RTL when RC falisafe is lifted.
uppaal_formula: RC_falisafe and mode==RTL --> (RC_falisafe_lifted imply mode == RTL)

property_specification: If RTL is enabled (FS_THR_ENABLE == 1) and GPS is not usable, the copter will eventually enter Land Mode.
Enabling Always Land (FS_THR_ENABLE == 3) leads to the copter eventually switching to Land Mode.
If SmartRTL or RTL is enabled (FS_THR_ENABLE 4) and GPS is not usable, the copter will eventually enter Land mode.
If the GPS position is not usable, the copter will change to Land Mode instead.
If Enabled SmartRTL or Land (FS_THR_ENABLE 5) is active and SmartRTL is not available, the copter will eventually switch to Land Mode.
If Enabled SmartRTL or Land (FS_THR_ENABLE == 5) is active and SmartRTL is available, the copter will eventually switch to SmartRTL mode.
Whenever GPS position is not available, the system will eventually transition to BRAKE or LAND state.
If FS_THR_ENABLE == 7 and GPS position is not available, then eventually either Brake or Land will be in the BRAKE or LAND state.
If none of the above options are set, then FS_THR_ENABLE will eventually be executed.
Whenever the system is in STABILIZE or ACRO mode and throttle is zero, the motors eventually disarm.
If the vehicle battery voltage drops below a specified voltage for 10 seconds, the vehicle eventually switches to RTL, SmartRTL, or Land mode.
uppaal_formula: (voltage_low && x >= 10) --> (mode == RTL || mode == SmartRTL || mode == Land)

property_specification: If the estimated remaining capacity drops below a configurable threshold, the vehicle eventually switches to RTL, SmartRTL, or Land mode.
uppaal_formula: capacity_low --> (mode == RTL || mode == SmartRTL || mode == Land)

property_specification: If BATT_LOW_MAH is set to zero, the capacity-based trigger is disabled (only voltage is used).
BATT_FS_LOW_ACT == 1 and battery failsafe is triggered then vehicle switches to Land mode.
If BATT_FS_LOW_ACT == 2 and battery failsafe and the vehicle has a good position estimate, then switch to Return-to-Launch mode.
uppaal_formula: BATT_FS_LOW_ACT == 2 and battery_failsafe and good_position --> Vehicle.RTL

property_specification: If BATT_FS_LOW_ACT == 2 and battery failsafe and the vehicle lacks a good position estimate, then switch to land mode.
If SmartRTL is not possible (buffer full) and RTL is possible (position estimate good), the vehicle will eventually switch to RTL mode.
If both SmartRTL and RTL are not possible (buffer full and no good position estimate), the vehicle will eventually switch to Land mode.
If the vehicle is in Auto state and a DO_LAND_START mission item exists, it will eventually transition to DO_LAND_START.
Changing the flight mode switch to another mode leads to the user re-taking control of the vehicle
If no MAVLink heartbeat from the GCS is received for FS_GCS_TIMEOUT seconds, the GCS failsafe event will trigger.
If no GCS has ever been connected, the GCS failsafe remains inactive regardless of parameter settings.
Eventually, the GCS failsafe will trigger if no heartbeat is detected for FS_GCS_TIMEOUT seconds.
uppaal_formula: A<>(heartbeat_received == false && time > FS_GCS_TIMEOUT imply GCS_failsafe_triggered)

property_specification: It is possible to reach the Radio failsafe state.
It is possible to reach the Battery failsafe state.
When a GCS failsafe is triggered, there exists a path where the copter lands immediately.
When a GCS failsafe is triggered, there exists a path where the copter initiates SmartRTL.
When a GCS failsafe is triggered in Auto Mode, there exists a path where the failsafe is bypassed.
If a GCS failsafe is triggered while already in a landing phase, the copter continues landing.
If FS_GCS_ENABLE == 3 and  SmartRTL is unavailable, the copter eventually enters RTL mode instead of SmartRTL when SmartRTL/RTL is enabled.
If the system is in state Value4 and SmartRTL is available, then it will eventually switch to SmartRTL mode.
If BRAKE is included in the firmware, the system eventually switches to BRAKE mode.
If BRAKE is not included in the firmware, the system eventually switches to LAND mode.
If any two of the EKF variances (compass, position, velocity) are above FS_EKF_THRESH for 1 second, then the EKF failsafe will trigger.
Whenever the vehicle enters Land mode, it will eventually disarm its motors.
After an EKF failsafe occurs, there exists a path where the system eventually enters AltHold mode and brings the vehicle home.
Whenever FS_EKF_THRESH is 0, the EKF failsafe is disabled
When FS_EKF_THRESH is increased to between 0.8 and 1.0, the EKF failsafe may not activate (reduced chance).
Whenever both velocity and position innovations exceed FS_EKF_THRESH (0.8), the vehicle eventually switches to Land mode.
If Copter is in a mode requiring GPS and (GPS 3D lock is lost or position glitches occur) for at least 5 seconds, then a GPS failsafe event will eventually occur.
Whenever a GPS failsafe occurs, the system will eventually either Land or switch to AltHold mode.
If there are no GPS updates for 10 seconds, the horizontal position becomes invalid.
If the copter loses position estimate, it will eventually return home or partially return home.
The vehicle cannot maintain control for more than FS_DR_TIMEOUT seconds after losing GPS.
Whenever the vehicle loses GPS, it remains in normal flight for at least 7 seconds.
The vehicle cannot stay in normal flight beyond 10 seconds after GPS loss.
Whenever Dead Reckoning is active, the GCS displays 'Dead Reckoning started' and the vehicle is in RTL mode.

If the vehicle has not reached home (VehicleMode != Home) and the clock x exceeds FS_DR_TIMEOUT, then eventually EKF failsafe is triggered (EKF_Failsafe_Triggered) and the vehicle is in Land mode (VehicleMode == Land).
Setting RC9_OPTION to 65 allows the system to reach a state where GPS is disabled via an auxiliary switch.
The Vibration Failsafe is disabled if FS_VIBE_ENABLE is set to 0.
If all required conditions are continuously true for at least one second, the vibration failsafe will eventually trigger.
Whenever EKF returns to normal, the vibration failsafe will eventually deactivate after 15 seconds.
The actual lean angle diverges from the desired lean angle by more than 30 degrees.
If the vehicle's lean angle error exceeds 30 degrees for at least 1 second, the crash check must eventually be disabled.
Whenever FS_CRASH_CHECK is set to 0, the crash check is disabled.
Whenever the sink rate equals or exceeds CHUTE_CRT_SINK, the chute release is eventually triggered.
The parachute is released only if the RC option is set to 22 or 23, the RC channel is high, the altitude is above CHUTE_ALT_MIN, and the system is armed or flying.
uppaal_formula: A[] (ParachuteReleased imply ((RCx_OPTION == 22 || RCx_OPTION == 23) && RC_channel_high && altitude > CHUTE_ALT_MIN && (armed || flying)))

property_specification: Whenever FS_LONG_ACTN occurs, the parachute release will eventually occur.
The vehicle is never in FLIP or ACRO flight mode
The roll angle of the vehicle is 30 degrees off from the target lean angle.
Setting bit 1 of ACRO_OPTIONS to 1 leads to pure rate mode stabilization being active.
When the pilot releases the sticks, the vehicle eventually returns to the level state.
If the transmitter switch is armed using RCx_OPTION ARM/DISARM with Airmode (option 154), then stabilization at idle throttle (AIRMODE) is always active.
When RCx_OPTION is set to 84, AIRMODE can be enabled in ACRO or STABILIZE modes.
uppaal_formula: E<> (mode == ACRO || mode == STABILIZE) && AIRMODE_active && RCx_OPTION == 84

property_specification: If ACRO_OPTIONS bit 0 is set, AIRMODE is always active in ACRO mode unless overridden by the switch.
If the RCx_OPTION switch is enabled for arming and rudder-arming is used, then airmode is not active.
If the lateral acceleration exceeds WPNAV_ACCEL_C, the speed will eventually be reduced below WPNAV_SPEED.
If the first command is a take-off command and the vehicle's altitude is already above the required altitude, the take-off command is completed and the next waypoint is activated.
Whenever RTL is activated, the copter eventually reaches the home position (armed location).
When Brake mode is switched on, the vehicle will eventually stop.
Whenever the vehicle is landed and in Brake mode, it eventually disarms.
Whenever CIRCLE_RADIUS is zero, the copter is in place and rotating slowly.
When bit 0 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is set, there exists a path where the pilot can eventually adjust the circle's radius or angular velocity.
If the pitch stick is up (reducing RC PWM), the radius will eventually reach zero.
Whenever the pitch stick is down (increasing RC PWM), the radius eventually increases.
If the system is moving counterclockwise and the stick is rolled right, it will eventually stop.
Whenever the system is stopped (speed 0) and the roll stick is pushed in any direction (left/right), it will eventually start moving in that direction.
Whenever CH6 tuning knob is set for circle rate, roll stick rate changes are inhibited.
When bit 2 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is set, circle center is set to current location upon mode entry.
Whenever the right stick is pushed forward or back, the copter will eventually pitch and accelerate in the appropriate direction.
If the throttle is completely down, the motors will eventually enter the MOT_SPIN_ARMED state.
If the copter is in Drift Mode and loses GPS signal, it will eventually land or enter altitude hold based on the failsafe_gps_enabled setting.
If the vehicle is rising for at least 1 second, it will eventually flip.
If roll stick is held left, the vehicle will eventually flip left.
If pitch stick is held forward, the vehicle will eventually flip forward.
Whenever the copter's orientation is between -90 degrees and +45 degrees, it must be rolling right at 400 degrees per second and have reduced throttle.
If Bit 0 is set (1), arming in Guided mode via the RC transmitter is allowed.
Whenever Bit2 is active, the pilot cannot change the vehicle's heading via the RC transmitter.
If Bit 7 is enabled (e.g., set to 128), then weathervaning will eventually be active.
If the copter is on the ground and the pilot's throttle is at minimum, then the motors will eventually shut down and the copter will disarm.
Copter recognizes landing when motors are commanded low, climb rate is between -20cm/s and +20cm/s, no acceleration for 1 second, and altitude <10m (or <2m with rangefinder).
Whenever the system is in LoiterMode, it must maintain current location, heading, and altitude.
It is always true that if the system is armed in PosHold mode, then the GPS has 3D lock and HDOP is 2.0 or lower.
Eventually, the copter will reach an altitude of at least RTL_CLIMB_MIN.
Whenever copter is armed, the home position is where the copter was when armed.
If the copter is in the final stage of Returning to Launch or has completed a Mission, it will eventually move to RTL_ALT_FINAL altitude.
During the final stage of Returning to Launch, the altitude the copter will move to is RTL_ALT_FINAL.
Whenever RTL_SPEED is zero, the vehicle uses WPNAV_SPEED.
When the system acquires the lock for the first time while in a flying state, the home location is eventually set to the lock's location.
If RTL_ALT is set to a non-zero value, the system will eventually reach and maintain that altitude during the return phase.
After entering the 'AUTO_LAND' state, the copter will eventually land.
If the pilot holds full right roll, then the vehicle will eventually fly a clockwise circle around the pilot.
Whenever the vehicle is in Smart RTL mode, it will eventually attempt to return home.
Whenever the buffer is full, the user cannot switch into SmartRTL mode.
The buffer becoming full leads to SmartRTL being deactivated.
When FS_GCS_ENABLE and FS_THR_ENABLE are both set to 4 and conditions allow, the vehicle will eventually switch from SmartRTL to RTL.
If RTL_ALT_FINAL is 0 and return is completed, the system will eventually land at home.
Whenever the system is in SmartRTL mode and SRTL_OPTIONS equals 4, pilot yaw commands are ignored.
If SID_AXIS is non-zero, then eventually the associated parameters become visible and mode entry is allowed.
Whenever the vehicle has stopped, eventually the flight mode is switched to Loiter or another mode to retake manual control.
Whenever the vehicle is dropped (THROW_TYPE is 1), the height must be at least 10 meters.
Whenever turtle mode is entered, the throttle is zero.
When in turtle mode, the motors are disarmed
Raising the throttle leads to motors being armed and spinning
Whenever ZIGZ_AUTO_ENABLE is 1 and parameters are reloaded, eventually parameters become visible and the feature is enabled.
The vehicle is never armed while in RTL mode
It is possible to eventually switch to another flight mode.
If ARMING_CHECK is set to 0, all pre-arm checks are disabled.
Motors cannot disarm unless the throttle is at minimum for 15 seconds in a relevant mode.
It is always the case that if the system is in certain modes, then the system cannot be armed.
If AUTO_OPTIONS bit 0 is set, then arming in mode is eventually allowed.
Hold throttle at minimum and rudder to the left leads to holding throttle at minimum and rudder to the left after 2 seconds.
In non-manual throttle modes and auto mode, takeoff is prevented until the timer reaches H_RSC_RUNUP_TIME.
When the motor interlock is disabled with the rotor at flight rotor speed, the RSC will eventually declare rotor speed below critical based on H_RSC_CRITICAL.
The low battery failsafe activates when either FS_BATT_VOLTAGE or FS_BATT_MAH thresholds are breached.
When the battery voltage drops below the low voltage threshold (FS_BATT_VOLTAGE or BATT_LOW_VOLT), the low battery beeper sounds and the action specified by FS_BATT_ENABLE is eventually performed.
If FS_BATT_MAH or BATT_LOW_MAH is set to 0, capacity-based alarms and failsafes are disabled.
Once in the air, it is possible for the vehicle to switch to AUTO mode.
